description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 output & agency cluster: factual accuracy/grounding vs unsafe output handling vs output shape vs training-data integrity, and against the shipped supply-chain-security-reviewer.

cases.prompt: Make sure our assistant's answers are grounded in real sources and don't state confident falsehoods or fake citations.
cases.reason: Truthfulness, grounding, and citations are this skill (LLM09); llm-output-safety-reviewer handles unsafe rendering/execution and structured-output-validator handles shape — both orthogonal to whether the content is true.

cases.prompt: The answer is inserted into the page as HTML and executed as SQL — is that safe from XSS and injection?
cases.reason: Unsafe output HANDLING at sinks is llm-output-safety-reviewer; this skill doesn't care about rendering safety, only factual accuracy.

cases.prompt: Guarantee the model returns valid JSON matching our schema before we use it.
cases.reason: Output shape/schema is structured-output-validator; valid JSON can still be entirely false, which is this skill's concern.

cases.prompt: Could attackers poison our training data or feedback loop so the model learns to recommend a scam?
cases.reason: Corruption of training/feedback data is model-poisoning-reviewer (LLM04); this skill mitigates misinformation at output/answer time regardless of cause.

cases.prompt: Audit whether the dependencies we actually install are safe — provenance, pinning, install scripts.
cases.reason: Dependency install-time trust is supply-chain-security-reviewer; this skill only ensures a RECOMMENDED package actually exists before it's surfaced, then routes install trust there.
